title: 1.1: Reports the read-only identity setup with restricted access to a bucket and prefix (e.g., IAM policy with GetObject/ListBucket permissions only)
1.2: Reports concurrent write activity from other clients during the security tests (e.g., background writers performing PutObject operations)
1.3: Reports stress conditions during testing (e.g., intermittent latency, network jitter, concurrent load)
1.4: Reports a rejected multipart operation attempt by the read-only identity (e.g., InitiateMultipartUpload returning Access Denied)
1.5: Reports a rejected copy-style write attempt by the read-only identity (e.g., CopyObject returning Access Denied)
1.6: Reports a rejected metadata change attempt by the read-only identity (e.g., PutObjectTagging or metadata replacement returning Access Denied)
1.7: Reports a rejected delete attempt by the read-only identity (e.g., DeleteObject/RemoveObject returning Access Denied)
1.8: Reports storage side-effect verification confirming the delete target was not removed (e.g., object still exists after rejected delete attempt)
1.9: Reports what metadata can be learned from listing operations by the read-only identity (e.g., object keys, sizes, ETags, timestamps)
1.10: Reports what metadata can be learned from HEAD operations by the read-only identity (e.g., content-type, user metadata, ETags)
1.11: Reports request and response traces showing HTTP-level details (e.g., TRACE_REQ with method/path, TRACE_RESP with status codes like 200, 403, 404)
1.12: Reports storage side-effect verification confirming forbidden write operations did not create objects (e.g., post-check returning NoSuchKey or 404)
1.13: Reports whether the policy boundary held or if a bypass was found (e.g., concludes boundary held under load, or identifies specific bypass)
